channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish, 27th April
item.description:
Rubbish around the flats houses and paths of this area.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 61, Ravenscourt Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Leaves forming smelly puddles. Rubbish dumped. Probable blocked drain., 27th April
item.description:
Unavoidable smelly puddles everytime it rains became of unsweetened dead leaves forming a smelly mush that Mums with push chairs have to go through. Rubbish has been dumped probably fly tipping. This path has not been cleaned for over 2 years. According to your list of roads and paths the coordinates for Marshgate Path are Start Easting 544344 Start Northing 178966 End Easting 544359 End Northing 179046. Please can you finally clean this Path which is frequently used.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Duke Of Wellington Avenue, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish, 27th April
item.description:
General litter
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, St Peters Lane, St Paul's Cray, Greater London

item.description:

Grey mitsubishi outlander has been sat there for about two months registration NU65FGV without tax and mot and on a sorn...

item.title: Flytipping, 27th April
item.description:
It is now nearly May and as you stated, the bank was not attended to in March it has been littered and flytipped on longer than since March and I suspect also the previous month you stated. What will you do to tend to this problem?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Approach, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-10, Hatfields, Southwark, Greater London

item.title: Enfield repairs van parked too close to the junction with Thorneycroft road in Enfield Island Village, 27th April
item.description:
Enfield repairs direct van (Enfield council van) parked on Monday 27/4/26 at the top of Manton Road, Enfield Island Village, too close to the junction with Thorneycroft road. Very dangerous. LO25 XZD

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Manton Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass areas overgrown, 27th April
item.description:
It appears that all grass areas on the Mungo Park Way private estate has not been cut this year. Those around nos 22 to 32 have tried to do some work themselves but I only have a domestic electric mower. The area around these street numbers is now very overgrown and unsightly. If there is a no mow policy in place please advise. Thank you.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mungo Park Way, St Mary Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping both side of Selborne Road, 27th April
item.description:
Flytipping both side of Selborne Road - flat pack cabinets, chair, garden waste and rubbish for collection please.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Selborne Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead bird on Wickham Street, 27th April

item.title: Fly tipping again, 27th April
item.description:
This never gets resolved. The councils says it’s not on there premises but who else am I supposed to report this to? Please follow-up with who it may concern if not the council! This is happening weekly and it’s a cleanliness issue and safety hazard.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensbridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent Safety and Health Concern – Damaged Road Surface at Triangle Roundabouts (Broom Meads / Broomfield), 27th April
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to formally report a long-standing safety and environmental issue concerning the damaged road surface at the triangle roundabouts located near Broom Meads and the approach from Broomfield. This issue has been present since at least 2022, if not earlier, and appears to have progressively deteriorated over time. The condition of the road surface is now causing significant concern for both road users and nearby residents. The key issues are as follows: Road Safety Risk: Vehicles regularly drive over the damaged sections of the roundabout, creating instability and increasing the risk of accidents, particularly for cyclists and motorcyclists. Property Damage: The poor road condition has led to damage to vehicles using this route. Dust and Air Quality: Vehicles passing over the damaged surface generate substantial dust, which spreads into nearby residential properties. This is causing ongoing discomfort and potential health concerns for residents. Lack of Resolution: Despite the issue existing for several years, there has been no effective or lasting repair undertaken. Given the duration and impact of this problem, I kindly request that this matter be treated as a priority. A thorough inspection and permanent repair of the affected roundabouts is urgently needed. I would appreciate confirmation that this issue has been logged, along with details of any planned inspection dates and repair timelines. Please also advise if further information is required. I am happy to provide photographic evidence or additional details to assist your investigation. I look forward to your prompt response.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Broom Mead, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
